speaker: POWELL
text: Good afternoon. Let me start with an assessment of the economy.

speaker: POWELL
text: Recent data point to a labor market that has come into better balance.

speaker: POWELL
text: Indeed, the labor market has shown clear signs of softening.

speaker: POWELL
text: Hiring has slowed and the unemployment rate has ticked up.

speaker: POWELL
text: Downside risks to employment have risen in recent months.

speaker: POWELL
text: Inflation has eased substantially from its peak toward our two percent goal.

speaker: POWELL
text: We are prepared to adjust the stance of policy as appropriate.

speaker: REPORTER
text: Mr. Chair — is the committee behind the curve on jobs?

speaker: POWELL
text: I'll say this plainly: the time to support the labor market is now, not later.

speaker: POWELL
text: We do not want to wait for material weakening to become the baseline.

speaker: POWELL
text: That said, we remain data-dependent and will move carefully.

speaker: POWELL
text: Policy is not on a preset course.

speaker: POWELL
text: I would not want to prejudge the outcome of this meeting.

speaker: REPORTER
text: But if the jobs numbers deteriorate from here?

speaker: POWELL
text: If the labor market were to weaken further, we would respond.

speaker: POWELL
text: Our tools are well positioned to act, and we will use them.

speaker: POWELL
text: I can tell you a number on the committee saw a cut as appropriate today.

speaker: POWELL
text: The discussion reflected real concern about the employment side of the mandate.

speaker: POWELL
text: We are attentive to the risks on both sides of our mandate.

speaker: POWELL
text: And we are committed to returning inflation to target over time.

speaker: POWELL
text: Thank you. I'll take a few more questions.
